channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: You Could Be Next
item.description: The LinkedIn post seemed like yet another scam job offer, but Katya was desperate enough to click. After college, she’d struggled to make a living as a freelance journalist, gone to grad school,...

item.title: I built MLShip — deploy your Streamlit or Gradio ML app in 60 seconds. No Docker. No AWS.
item.description: I am a CS student who built something and I am trying to make it better by talking to the right people. That is it. MLShip is a one-click deployment platform for Streamlit and Gradio ML apps. The...

item.title: SQL Joins and Window Functions
item.description: 1. JOINS Sometimes it is very difficult to look through data to get specific things. For example, you want the name of customers who made purchases as well as the name of the products they bought....

item.title: Zero-Friction Publishing: A Human-in-the-Loop Agentic CMS powered by Notion MCP
item.description: This is a submission for the Notion MCP Challenge What I Built Me: I intend to submit to the Notion MCP challenge. Will the workflow below be a good application? Claude: This is a strong...

item.title: The AI Cold Start That Breaks Kubernetes Autoscaling
item.description: Autoscaling usually works extremely well for microservices. When traffic increases, Kubernetes spins up new pods and they begin serving requests within seconds . But AI inference systems behave...

item.title: Data Paradigms for the Agent Era: From Human-Readable to AI-Understandable
item.description: HTML, PDF, JSON — every data format in existence was designed for humans to read. When AI Agents become the primary data consumers, we need to rethink from first principles: what format is best...

item.title: Building Multi-Agent Slack Routing with OpenClaw
item.description: As AI systems evolve, a single assistant is often not enough. Many workflows benefit from multiple specialized agents — each with its own personality, capabilities, and routing logic. Recently I...

item.title: Understanding Core React Hooks with Practical Examples
item.description: “React Hooks turned complex class components into simple, reusable functions.” Key Takeaways React Hooks replaced class components as the standard way to write React. useState, useEffect, and...

item.title: Getting Started with SQL subqueries. A complete beginner guide with examples.
item.description: Introduction Imagine a family unit (parent(s) and children). Every child has their own unique traits i.e height, weight, voice, complexion etc... Now children are to parent(s) what subqueries are...

item.title: Introducing the AI Governance Glossary (v1.3.0): A Vocabulary for Behavioral AI Governance
item.description: AI governance discussions usually begin with models, agents, and automation. But complex systems rarely fail because of the technology alone. They fail because behavioral patterns shift faster...

item.title: Code doesn’t quit, but people do: Clean Code, Technical Debt, and the Bus Factor
item.description: TL;DR Technical debt isn’t just a developer inconvenience, it’s a business risk with compound interest. When developers leave, they often take the “why” behind the code with them. Clean Code isn’t...

item.title: Threat Actors Mass-Scan Salesforce Experience Cloud via Modified AuraInspector Tool
item.description: Salesforce has warned of an increase in threat actor activity that's aimed at exploiting misconfigurations in publicly accessible Experience Cloud sites by making use of a customized version of an...

item.title: CISA Flags SolarWinds, Ivanti, and Workspace One Vulnerabilities as Actively Exploited
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added three security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active...

item.title: Yann LeCun’s AMI Labs raises $1.03 billion to build world models
item.description: AMI Labs, the new venture cofounded by Turing Prize winner Yann LeCun after he left Meta, has raised $1.03 billion at a $3.5 billion pre-money valuation.

item.title: Yann LeCun Raises $1 Billion to Build AI That Understands the Physical World
item.description: Meta’s former chief AI scientist has long argued that human-level AI will come from mastering the physical world, not language. His new startup, AMI, aims to prove it.

item.title: Nvidia Is Planning to Launch an Open-Source AI Agent Platform
item.description: Ahead of its annual developer conference, Nvidia is readying a new approach to software that embraces AI agents similar to OpenClaw.

item.title: Apple smart home display rumors now point to a fall launch with iOS 27
item.description: The rumored "HomePod with a screen" we've heard so much about was reportedly lined up for launch in 2025, and then this spring, and now, according to the latest updates, it's on the shelf until...

item.title: The government shutdown is hitting airports — but not ICE
item.description: Chaos reigned at airports across the country last weekend, with thousands of travelers reportedly waiting in hours-long security lines thanks to staffing shortages. Transportation Security...

item.title: Everything from the last week of everything is gambling now
item.description: Kalshi is facing backlash from its own users after a class action lawsuit was filed last week alleging it failed to pay out as promised for wagers regarding when Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah...

item.title: OpenAI and Google employees rush to Anthropic’s defense in DOD lawsuit
item.description: More than 30 OpenAI and Google DeepMind employees signed onto a statement supporting Anthropic's lawsuit against the Defense Department after the agency labeled the AI firm a supply-chain risk,...

item.title: Anthropic Claims Pentagon Feud Could Cost It Billions
item.description: Executives at the AI startup say companies paused deal talks after the Trump administration labeled it a supply-chain risk, warning that the fallout could cause a major revenue hit.

item.title: Employees across OpenAI and Google support Anthropic’s lawsuit against the Pentagon
item.description: On Monday, Anthropic filed its lawsuit against the Department of Defense over being designated as a supply chain risk. Hours later, nearly 40 employees from OpenAI and Google - including Jeff...

item.title: OpenAI and Google Workers File Amicus Brief in Support of Anthropic Against the US Government
item.description: Google DeepMind chief scientist Jeff Dean is among the AI researchers and engineers rushing to Anthropic's defense.

item.title: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber will step aside
item.description: Jay Graber became the first CEO of Bluesky in 2021 when the network and its AT Protocol spun out from life as a Twitter research project to go independent, but now she's leaving that role, as...

item.title: You can get three months of Disney Plus and Hulu for $15
item.description: If you’re looking for a way to stream the Oscars this weekend, or catch up on nominees like The Secret Agent, the Disney Plus and Hulu bundle is worth a look. The ceremony will stream live on Hulu...

item.title: Apple’s new M5 Max feels like a huge upgrade if you bought your laptop three years ago
item.description: We've been busy testing many new MacBooks, ranging from the new $1,099 M5 MacBook Air, going all the way up the $6,149 16-inch MacBook Pro with the M5 Max chip. While these computers are identical...

item.title: Apple Studio Display XDR review: pro at a premium
item.description: It's been almost exactly four years since Apple released the 5K Studio Display that so many wanted, even if it didn't really deliver as a high-end display. Apple has now revamped the Studio...

item.title: One of this rugged phone’s cameras is a pop-out action cam
item.description: What if your smartphone's camera wasn't locked to the back of the device? Honor's Robot Phone, which we got to see in action at MWC 2026, features a camera attached to a stabilized gimbal arm that...

item.title: Anthropic launches code review tool to check flood of AI-generated code
item.description: Anthropic launched Code Review in Claude Code, a multi-agent system that automatically analyzes AI-generated code, flags logic errors, and helps enterprise developers manage the growing volume of...

item.title: Granite 4.0 1B Speech: Compact, Multilingual, and Built for the Edge
